questions.text: What do Query (Q), Key (K), and Value (V) represent in attention?
options: Query retrieves information, Key identifies content, Value contains the actual data
Query is input, Key is hidden layer, Value is output
Query is embedding, Key is weight, Value is bias
They are three different types of layers
questions.correctAnswer: Query retrieves information, Key identifies content, Value contains the actual data

questions.text: What is the purpose of scaling by √d_k in the attention formula?
options: To prevent gradients from becoming too small
To normalize the dot product so softmax doesn't saturate
questions.correctAnswer: To normalize the dot product so softmax doesn't saturate

options: Running attention multiple times in sequence
Running multiple attention operations in parallel, then concatenating results
questions.correctAnswer: Running multiple attention operations in parallel, then concatenating results

questions.text: How does softmax in the attention mechanism create a probability distribution? Why is this important?
Why is causal masking applied in language model attention?
options: To prevent looking at future tokens during generation
questions.correctAnswer: To prevent looking at future tokens during generation

questions.text: How many attention heads does Gemma-4 typically have?
Explain what would happen if attention weights were not normalized (i.e., if we skipped the softmax step). How would this affect the model?
